def: s.m. Armpit, side, flank (see kāṅkh).
s.f. A waistband, girdle; the end of the lower garment, which, after the cloth is carried round the body, is brought up behind and tucked into the waistband.
s.m. A paternal uncle ( = kākā, q.v.); — sarcasm. s.f. Cawing (of a crow, &c.)
s.f. Space, region, quarter (as east, west, &c.).
s.m. Summit (of a mountain); hump (of a bull, &c.); an ensign or symbol of royalty (as the white parasol, &c.).
s.f. A curl, lock, ringlet; a tuft of hair left on the top of the head
more: Poet. n. f. A long curl or ringlet ( zulf ). kākuleṅ chhoṛnā , v. a. To hang down one's curls.
s. f. A curl, lock, ringlet.

def: s.m. Large cardamoms.
s.m. A fine kind of ermine.

def: s.m. A saying, a word; loquaciousness; — boasting, egotism
more: (Heb. ) s. m. 1. A word, a saying. 2. Loquaciousness. 3. Boasting, egotism. قال مارنا ḳāl mārnā, 1. To be very loquacious. 2. To rebuke, to reproach. قال مقال ḳāl maḳāl, s. m. Altercation, wrangling, confabulation.
subs: v.n. To talk, prate; to be very loquacious; — (with loc.) to rebuke, reproach
or qīl-o-qāl, s.m. Confabulation; logomachy; altercation, wrangling, squabbling.

def: s.m. Bad or inauspicious time; dearth, famine; calamity; — adj. Bad, inauspicious; unseasonable
more: n. m. See زمانه (1, 2, 4). 1. Season. 2. Dearth; famine ( akāl , 2.). Kāl, karhau, kisāṅṛ kā khau . Rus. Prov. Famine and murrain the peasant's life-blood drain. 3. Angel of death; death. 1. Are! tanne de dūṅ kāl ke mauṅḍe! mat phūṅk! Rus. Wom. You brat! I'll drive you into the jaws of death! dont put me in a rage! 2. Akāl nahīṅ hai, kāl hai . Prov. Not Famine but black Death it is. kāl-upkār, kāl meṅ kaṅglā pālan kā rupayā . Famine relief fund. kāl ānā , v. n. To come (one's time or death). kāl batīt h., yā kaṭnā , v. n. 1. To pass (time). 2. To pass away (a famine). kāl banjar , n. f. Land left fallow for a considerable time. kāl paṛnā , v. n. To come upon the land (famine). kāl kā kām , n. m. Famine work. kāl-kā-mārā, kāl-kā-ṭūṭā , adj. Famine-stricken. kāl kar jānā , v. n. To go the way of all flesh. kāl-koṭhṛī , n. f. 1. A black-hole. 2. Solitary imprisonment. kāl ke bas h. , v. n. …

adj. S. काला Cont. of kālā black. Great; large. kāl-juārī , n. m. A desperate gambler. kāl-rātrī , n. f. 1. A very dark night. 2. The 7th of the 7th month of the 77th year, after which a man is exempt from the observance of religious ordinances. kāl-ganḍait' , n. m. A cobra with white spots.

s. m. 1. Death. 2. ( met. ) A snake. 3. Time, season, age. کال بتانا يا کاٿنا يا گنوانا kāl-bitānā, or -kāṭnā, or gaṅwānā, To waste oneʼs time, to spend time. کال بس هونا kāl-bas honā, To be in the hands of death, to be seized by fate. 4. Dearth, famine. کال پڙنا kāl paṛnā, The coming on of a famine, to happen a famine. 5. Angel of death. 6. Calamity. 7. (in Braj for कल्य ) To-morrow.
subs: Famine to happen; the coming on of famine
or kāl-kā mārā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Famine-stricken; — a victim of famine; a starveling

def: adv. (Braj, & rustic) To-morrow, &c. ( = kal, q.v.). s.m. A saying, a word; loquaciousness; — boasting, egotism
subs: v.n. To talk, prate; to be very loquacious; — (with loc.) to rebuke, reproach
or qīl-o-qāl, s.m. Confabulation; logomachy; altercation, wrangling, squabbling.

def: s.m. & adv. The morrow, to-morrow; yesterday ("in the Dakkhanī dialect kal is used only in the sense of 'yesterday'; 'to-morrow' is expressed by ṣabāṅ or ṣubāṅ," corr. fr. A. ṣabāḥ, or ṣubḥ)
more: adj. Black. Cont. of کالا q. v. kal-sirā , adj. lit . black-headed. Hence, A man. Kalsirā baṛā āfat hai . kal-muī , n. f. kal-muā , n. m. 1. A black-faced woman. 2. A forbidding countenance.

n. f. S. कल्य Pr. kallam dawn. Tomorrow (Sant. gapā ); yesterday (Sant, holā ). In the Dakhanī dialect kal is used only in the sense of yesterday, Tomorrow is expressed by sabāṅ or subāṅ . Binning. 1. Kal kis ne dekhā hai ( yā kal kī kise khabar ). Prov. Who hath seen tomorrow? 2. Jo āj devegā, yahāṅ vaisā hī woh kal pāvegā, Kal devegā kal pāvegā, kalpāvegā kalpāvegā . Give here today, tomorrow you as good shall get, Nazīr. Give ease, get ease; give pain, you suffer pain and fret. 3. Āj hai so kal nahīṅ . Prov. What is today tomorrow is not. kal kī bāt , n. f. 1. A matter of yesterday. 2. Something that happened very lately. kalkalāṅ ko , adv. 1. In future. kalkalāṅ ko mukar na jānā! 2. Perchance; by chance. kalkalāṅ ko , conj. Lest; with a view to prevent.

n. f. 1. Side; flank. Dekhiye ūṅṭ kis kal baiṭhtā hai ? Prov. We shall see on which side the camel squats. ☞ A potter and a green grocer hired a camel between them for carrying their pots and greens respectively. On the way the camel made free with the grocer's greens, whereat the potter rejoiced that his pots were safe. At the journey's end however the tables were turned; for, as the camel reclined to that side crack went all the pots. 2. Rest ( ārām , 1—3). 3. ( कला ) A machine, or a part of one. 4. A trigger; the hammer (of a g/?/) kal be-kal h., kal se be-kal h. , v. n. To be disj/?/ted or out of joint; to have a loose screw. kal paṛnā, ānā, yā pānā , v. n. To be at ease. kal ṭhīk k., yā sudhārnā , To regulate or repair a machine. kal-dār , n. m. 1. Coin with a milled edge. 2. A rupee; money. Rupayā sikka-i-kaldār . A rupee stamped with a device and having a milled rim. kal-dār bandū…

1. ( कल्य ) s. m. To morrow, yesterday. کلکي بات kal-kī bāt, News of yesterday, something that happened very lately. 2. s. f. Ease, tranquillity, peace, relief, quiet, rest. کل مکل kal-makal, s. f. Uneasiness, disquiet, trouble. 3. ( कला ) s. f. A trap, a machine, a lock. کل کا آدمي kal-kā ādmī, An automaton, a puppet; ( met. ) a very weak person. کل کاگهوڙا kal-kā ghoṛā, A very well trained and obedient horse. 4. ( कलि ) The name of the fourth age. See کلجگ kali-jug.
subs: adj. (f. -kī), Of or relating to yesterday
A man of yesterday, novus homo, an upstart, a parvenu
A matter of yesterday, a recent occurrence
or kal-rāt-ko, Last night; to-morrow night
adv. & conj. In future; perchance; lest.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.f. A small part (of anything), part, side (e.g. dekhiye ūṅṭ kis kal baiṭhtā hai); — a machine, or a part of one; an instrument; a lock; a trap; a trigger, hammer (of a gun)
subs: adj. & s.m. Formed or wrought by machinery; having a milled rim (a coin); having a trigger (a gun); — coin with a milled edge, a rupee, &c.; a trigger-gun, an English gun (opp. to toṛe-dār, 'a matchlock')
adj. (f. -kī), Made by machinery; worked by machinery; mechanical
An automaton, a puppet; (met.) a very weak person
A workshop in which machinery is employed
A mechanical horse; a well-trained and obedient horse
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.f. Ease, tranquillity, peace, quiet, relief, rest, repose
subs: or kal paṛnā (-ko), or kal pānā, To obtain ease or relief; to be at ease
or kal-se be-kal honā, To be ill at ease, to be disturbed or disquieted, to lose all rest or peace
s.f. Uneasiness, perturbation, disquietude, distraction, trouble.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.f. = kalī or kali, q.v. s.f. A woollen carpet; tapestry ( = qālīn).
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.m. = کلي kali, q.v.
subs: kal-jug = kali-yug. s.f. A woollen carpet; tapestry
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: adj. & s.m. Scald-headed; bald; — a man who has wounds and scars on his head and has lost his hair; a bald-head.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Black, dark; — great, fearful, terrible; — a name of Krishṇ; the black and most venomous variety of the cobra, Coluber naga; — a sepoy
more: adj. 1. Black; dark; gloomy ( siyāh , 1). 1. Bhāī! kāle mānas sāban lāë gore nāṅh hoṅ . Rus. Prov. Soap wont wash a black man white. (Dark deeds cannot be hid). 2. Kālā mūṅh, nile hāth pāoṅ! Wom. A curse. May your face be black, your limbs a livid blue! 3. Ser ek pīve kuṭī tamākhū, aur ser ek pīve bhāṅg, Āp jāë baṅgle meṅ sove, hameṅ batāve bāṅjh, Chuṅdaryā le de moë, laharyā le de moë! maiṅ kālī par gaī . Rus. song. A seer of ground tobacco smokes, a seer of bhang beside, Apart he in his bungalow sleeps, my womb in vain decried, Coal black I'm turned, buy me, my spouse, a figured wrap bright-dyed! 2. See kāl. Kālā chor . A great thief. kālā , n. m. 1. A black snake; a cobra. 2. A sepoy. Zulm goroṅ ne kiyā, aur na sitam kāloṅ ne, Ham ko barbād kīyā apnī hī āmāloṅ ne . Shahrāshob. Sepoys nor English soldiers have oppressed us thus, Our own misdeeds have all this evil wrought on us. kālā-ā…

1. adj. Black, dark. کالا چور kālā chor, An unknown person. 2. s. m. Name of a snake. 3. Time. 4. Name of Kr̤ish̤ṇa. کالا بال kālā bāl, s. m. The lower part of the belly, the pubes. کالا بال اپنا جاننا يا سمجهنا kālā bāl apnā jānnā or samajhnā, To despise (any one) exceedingly. کالا دانه kālā dāna, Name of a purgative seed. (Convolvulus nil?). کالا جيره kālā jīra, or کالا زيره kālā zīra, s. m. Seeds of the Nigella indica: Roxb. کالا منـہہ کرنا kālā muṅh karnā, 1. To break off all connexion with another. 2. To disgrace. 3. To copulate. 4. To expel. کالا نمک kālā namak, s. m. A kind of rock-salt impregnated with sulphur and bitumen, which leaves an hepatick flavour in the mouth. (It is much used medicinally). کالي برن kāle baran, Of the colour of a snake. کالي کوس kāle kos, A great distance.
s. m. Silk cloths, (and, in general) any kind of household furniture. کالاي بد بريش خاوند kālā-e-bad ba-rīsh-i-ḵẖāwind, Let bad things be returned to their proprietor.
subs: or kāle-bāl, s.m. lit. 'Black hair or hairs,' the pubes; the groin; — kālā-bāl apnā jānnā or samajhnā (-ko), To hold (one) in no esteem, or in great contempt
s.m. A black snake; — adj. Black as a snake, very black, jet black
s.m. lit. 'Black water'; beyond the sea; transportation (across the sea)
s.m. A black mountain or hill; (met.) an elephant; — the head
s.m. A great thief; — an unknown person; a dark horse
s.m. Name of a purgative seed, Ipomæa cœrulea; indigo seed; woad, Convolvulus nil
s.m. The purple thorn-apple, Datura fastuosa purpurascens
s.m. A black demon; a great devil; — a very black man
or kālā jīrā, s.m. Seed of the Nigella indica
v.t. To make black, to blacken; to blot, scribble over, deface by scribbling
adj. (f. -ī), Black as a lamp-black box, coal-black, jet-black
s.m. A black crow, a raven

or muṅh kālā karnā (-kā), To blacken the face (of), to disgrace, bring disgrace (on); to turn out with disgrace, to expel; to break off all connexion (with); — to copulate (with); to violate, to ravish
or kālā non, s.m. A kind of black rock-salt, impregnated with sulphur and bitumen, which leaves a hepatic flavour in the mouth (it is much used medicinally)
s.f. 'A black calamity'; a great evil; a plague; an old hag
adj. Of the colour of a snake; of a black colour
s.f. A rich black soil, vegetable mould
v.n. To look very angry and threatening
s.f. Basil, Ocymum basilicum or sanctum
s.f. A variety of cowhage with a purplish stem, Dolichos (carpopogon) pruriens purpurascens; — kāle-kos, s.m. pl. 'Great distances,' a great distance
s.f. Black soil ( = kālī bhūmi); — black-lead
(-ke), 'To place a black pot on the head' (of); to bring disgrace (upon).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit काल (kāla), from Proto-Dravidian *kār.

def: s.m. Time ( = kāl, q.v.). s.m. A saying, a word; loquaciousness; — boasting, egotism
subs: v.n. To talk, prate; to be very loquacious; — (with loc.) to rebuke, reproach
or qīl-o-qāl, s.m. Confabulation; logomachy; altercation, wrangling, squabbling.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit काल (kāla), from Proto-Dravidian *kār.

def: s.f. Name of a daughter of Daksha (the mother of the Kālakeyas, a family of asurs or demons).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit काल (kāla), from Proto-Dravidian *kār.

def: s.m. = کاله kāla, q.v. s.m. A fort (esp. one on a mountain or an eminence), a fortress, castle, citadel, fortification
subs: To make a fortification, or an entrenchment
To build a fort; to fortify (a place)
adj. Shut up in a fort
To take refuge in a fort; to keep in a fortified place (an army)
vulg. qilʻe-dār, s.m. Governor of a fort, commandant of a garrison
s.f. The command of a garrison
s.f. A siege-gun; siege-battery; a very large cannon
s.f. The garrison of a fort.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit काल (kāla), from Proto-Dravidian *kār.

def: s.m. Silk-cloth; cloth; household furniture or utensils, effects, worldly goods, things ( = kālā).

s.f. A sixteenth part (of anything); a small part; part, portion, share; a digit or one-sixteenth of the moon's diameter; a particular division of time (about eight seconds); the sixtieth part of a degree, a minute; interest on capital (considered as a part of it); — any practical art, a mechanical or elegant art; accomplishment, skill, dexterity, ingenuity, art, contrivance, trick, juggling; — (in Hindū poetry) a symbol for the number sixteen
more: n. f. S. कला 1. A digit or 16th part of the moon's diameter; a minute; the 60th part of a degree. Par ghar kabahuṅ na jāiye, ghaṭat hai jot, Ravī manḍal meṅ jātu sisi chhīn kal chhabī hot . 2. Art; trick; accomplishment. Chham, chhamak, jhamak piyārī, paiṅjanī bajāve, aur hoṅṭhan adhar dhar phūṅk mārī baṅsurī, Jhanak jhanak jhaṭak jhaṭ naṭ kī kalā, kheleṅ tan taṛak taṛak tarkat vākī pasurī . Song. Jhūlnā . 3. Miraculous power. Kālkā debī meṅ baṛī kalā hai. kalā-baḍḍū . A tall stupid fellow. kalā jaṅg . A trick in wrestling. kalā k., yā khelnā , v. a. 1. See kalā k . 2. To play a trick. kalā na lagnā , v. n. Not to avail or succeed.

s. f. 1. A digit or sixteenth part of the moonʼs diameter. 2. A division of time, about eight seconds. 3. Part, portion. 4. Art, trick. کلا بازي kalā-bāz-ī, s. f. Turning over head and heels, tumbling, juggling. کلا نه بد نا kalā na badnā, v. a. To disobey, to decline, not to succeed. کلا نه لگنا kalā na lagnā, Not to have an advantage, not to avail.
subs: s.f. Tumbling, turning over head and heels, taking a somersault
To tumble, to take a somersault
(S. kalā+jaṅghā), s.f. An acrobatic feat; a trick in wrestling
s.m. 'A treasury or storehouse of digits,' the moon
To disobey, to decline; not to succeed (cf. next)
'A contrivance or device to fail of effect,' not to avail or succeed, not to have an advantage
vulg. kalāṅ-vat, or kalāṅ-vant, s.m. (f. -vatī), lit. 'Possessed of art or skill'; a class of singers or musicians; one of that class, a minstrel; — lit. 'having digits'; the moon.
wety: From Sanskrit कील (kīl).

def: visceral leishmaniasis
artist; artisan; actor
s. (dakh.) Phyllanthus multiflorus, Klein. (Qanoon-e-islam, glossary, p. lxxxv).

n. m. lit . black waters. Beyond the sea; transportation beyond India; the Andaman islands. kālā-pan , n. m. Blackness. kālā-pahāṛ , n. m. lit . a black rock or hill. ( Met .) 1. An elephant. 2. The head. kālā til , n. m. Sesamum orientale , Wat. kālā-chor , n. m. lit . a black thief. An unknown person; a dark horse. kālā dānā , n. m. A purgative; Ipomæa cærulea , Wat. kālā dhatūrā , n. m. Thorn apple; datura fastuosa , Wat. kālā deo . A great devil; black man. kālā k. , v. a. 1. To blacken. 2. To blot; blotch; scribble. kālā kavvā , n. m. A black crow; a raven. kālā-koëlā ; Tir. kār-kouā , adj. Coal-black. kālā kīkar , n. m. Acacia arabica. kālā lobhiā. Dolichos catjung , Wat. kālā muṅh k. , More com. mūnh kālā k. , v. a. 1. To blacken one's face. 2. To disgrace; stigmatize; bring disgrace on. 3. To turn out with disgrace. 4. To break off all connexion with one. 5. To deflour; ravish. Bāqī hai Shaikh ko abhī hasrat gunāh kī, Mūṅh bhī karegā kālā jo ḍāṛhī siyāh kī . Zauq. The Shekh has still a mind to go the pace, His beard dyed black, he 'll blacken eke his face. kālā-namak, kālā-non , n. m. Black rocksalt impregnated with sulphur and bitumen, which leaves an hepatic flavour in the mouth. kāle sir kā , n. m. A man. kāle sir kā ek na chhoṛnā , v. a. ( Slang ) To go the rig. kal-sirā , n. m. kal-sirī , n. f. A black-headed Bird. kal-poṭyā , n. m. A black-chested bird. kal-dumā , n. m. A bird with a black tail. kal-mūṅhā , n. m. kal-mūṅhī , n. f. A blackfaced creature.

s.m. Time; (in Gram.) tense; season; age; right or proper time, fit season; meal-time; Time personified; destiny, fate; death; time of death; the messenger or angel of death; the regent of the dead, one of the names of Jam or Yama
subs: (-ko), Death to come, the last hour to come (to)
(kāla+an˚), s.m. Intervening time, interval; the lapse of time, the process of time, period; — another time; a more suitable time
To pass time; to spend time, to while away time; to waste time
adj. In the clutches or grasp of death; liable to death; seized by fate; — kāl-bas honā, v.n. To be in the clutches of death or fate, &c.
s.m. 'The dawning of the best season,' the two months following the rainy season; the sultry season; the autumn
vulg. kālagya, s.m. One who knows the fixed times or seasons; an astrologer
vulg. kāl-gyān, s.m. Worldly or secular knowledge, the knowledge of worldly things (as distinguished from things spiritual and eternal)
s.m. The wheel of time; a given revolution of time, a cycle, a period of time, an epoch
s.m. The law, rule, or operation of time; death, dying; — the influence of time; seasonableness

s.f. 'The night of all-destroying time'; a particular night in the life of man, on the seventh day of the seventh month of the seventy-seventh year (after which period a man is exempt from attending to the usual religious ordinances)
s.m. The thread of time or death; name of one of the twenty-one hells
v.n. To go the way of all flesh
s.m. The lapse of time, the process of time
adv. According to the process of time
s.m. Allowing time to pass away, delay, loss of time; spending or passing time; enduring suffering or affliction
v.n. To be subject to time and circumstances; to be in the hands of death ( = kāl-bas honā)
s.f. lit. 'Rope of death'; a snake
s.f. The felly or rim of the wheel of time'; a kind of terrible weapon; — name of an Asur or demon (slain by Krishṇ)
adj. (in Gram.) Denoting time, having reference to time, of time

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Black ( = kālā), of a dark colour, dark; dark-blue; — great, large, excessive, terrible; — a black or dark-blue colour; — the black part of the eye; — the black and most venomous variety of the Cobra, Coluber naga
subs: (˚la+an˚), s.m. A small sbrub used as a purgative
(˚la+ag˚), s.m. A black kind of aloe-wood or Agallochum; — kāl-juʼārī, s.m. A great or terrible gambler
or kāl-sarp, s.m. The black and most poisonous variety of the cobra ( = kāla, or kālā, or kālā sāṅp)
or kāl-ganḍait, s.m. A kind of cobra with white spots
s.m. A kind of black factitious and purgative salt ( = kālā lon, and kālā namak)
s.f. Bengal madder, Rubia munjista; — black Teorī, Ipomæa atropurpurea
s.f. The plant Vernonia (or Serratula, or Conyza) anthelmintica; — Bengal madder, Rubia munjista
s.m. A fragrant and resinous exudation from the plant Amyris agallocha; bdellium
vulg. kāl-yaman, s.m. Name of a prince of the Yavans; a tyrannical Asur, the foe of Krishṇ, destroyed by him by a stratagem.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Dumb; hoarse; inarticulate; soft, sweet (tone), low; weak; — a dumb person, &c.; — a low or soft, or inarticulate tone, humming, buzzing, &c.; — the Indian cuckoo ( = kokil)
more: n. m. The 4th or iron age.
subs: or kal-kal, s.m. A confused noise, murmuring or buzz (of a crowd), hum; wrangling, quarrel; — an epithet of Śiva
To make a confused noise; to wrangle, &c.
s.m. A pleasing tone or note; — adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Having a sweet voice or note; — a person who has a sweet voice; the Indian cuckoo ( = kokil); a dove, pigeon, &c.
s.m. (f. -ī), A kind of duck or goose; the whistling teal.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.m. The head; crown (of the head); top, upper part; upper leather (of a shoe or slipper); — cheek; jaw; gill (of a fish); throat; (met.) clamour, noise, loud speech; boasting
more: n. m. 1. The jaws; head; cheek. Kallā chale sattar balā ṭale . Prov. While the jaws work seventy ills are defeated. (While he eats he lives). 2. The throat. Is aurat kā baṛā kallā hai . 3. The head (of sheep, etc). kallah ba-kallah laṛnā , v. n. To bandy words with; to have a wordy duel. kalle tale dabānā , v. a. To drown one's voil. kallā-toṛ , adj. Jaw-breaking. def: s.m. = kalār, q.v.
more: n. m. kalāran . n. f. S. कलाद 1. A sect of Hindus whose profession originally was distilling spirits. 2. A distiller. 3. ( kalālī ) A seller of spirituous liquors; a publican; tavern-keeper. 1. Gaṅgā bahī jāe, kalāl kī beṭī chhātī pīṭe . Prov. The Ganges flows on the publican's daughter beats her breast. (She would have diluted the liquor with all this water). 2. Kalār kī dukān par pānī bhī pīyo to sharāb kā gumān hotā hai . Prov. Drink even water at a tavern and you are suspected of having drunk spirits. 3. Kalāl kī beṭī dūbne chalī, logoṅ ne kahā matvālī hai . Prov. The publican's daughter went to drown herself, the people said 'she's drunk.' kalāl jama . Revenue from the excise, or duty on spirits.
s. m. (dakh.) A distiller, vintner, innkeeper ( Harris ): see کلار kalār.
subs: s.f. Duty on spirituous liquors; revenue from the excise
s.m. A distillery; a liquor-shop, public-house, tavern.

ety: fr. qalm, 'paring,' inf. n. of قلم; cf. Gr. κάλαμος; Lat. calamus, and culmus; S. कलम
def: s.f. Cuttings (of trees, &c., for planting), a set, a slip; a section, paragraph (of a chapter in a book); — the upper part of the beard tapering to a point; a kind of firework; — a crystal (as of salts); — qalam uṭhā-kar likhnā, or qalam bar-dāshta likhnā, To write offhand
more: n. S. कलम; Lat. calamus . 1. A reed pen to write with. 2. A painter's brush. 1. Par kī qabam . A quill pen. 2. Surme kī qalam . A pencil. 3. Kāle mūṅh kī bhare kulāṅcheṅ, Ulṭī ho unglī par nāche, Jab kue meṅ māre ḍubbī Dil kā hāl batāve chuppī . Riddle. Ans. Qulam . Pen. Black-faced, it prances, dances on the finger upside down, Into the well it dips, and lo! its heart in silence shown. 3. A slip or cutting. 4. The upper part of the beard tapering to a point. 5. (E. murhā ) A kind of firework. 6. A crystal (as of salts). Shore yā billaur kī qalam . 7. ( Slang ) Membrum virile. qalam uṭhā-kar, yā qalam bar-dāshtah likhnā , v. n. To write off hand. qalam andāz k. , v. a. To omit, leave out (of a writing). qalam banānā , v. a. 1. To mend a pen. 2. To shave the upper part of the beard. qalam-band , adj. 1. Written; penned; written out. 2. Taken into account. 3. Inserted; entered. Phr. Under …

s. m. (by Hindūs s. f. Sans. कलम Gr. κάλαμος ) 1. A reed. 2. A pen. 3. A mode of writing characters, handwriting. 4. s. f. Cuttings of trees, &c. for planting, a set, a slip. 5. A kind of firework. قلم انداز ḳalam-andāz, Throwing away the pen. قلم بند کرنا ḳalam band karnā, To take down, to write. قلم بندي ḳalam-band-ī, s. f. Empire, sovereignty, jurisdiction. قلم کار ḳalam-kār, An engraver. قلم کاري ḳalam-kār-ī, s. f. Engraving. قلم کرنا ḳalam karnā, To cut. قلم کشي ḳalam-kash-ī, s. f. Writing. قلم لگانا ḳalam lagānā, To plant slips or cuttings.

def: n. f. Territories; domain ( rāj ). qalam-rau-i-angrez vāqe kind . G. G. British India. qalam-qasāī , n. m. lit . butchers with the pen. A cant term for clerks of the Courts. qalam-kār , n. m. 1. A quill-driver. 2. An engraver; a painter. qalam-kārī , n. f. 1. Writing. 2. Engraving; painting. qalam k. , v. a. To cut; cut off; prune. 1. Pahle chor ke hāth qalam kīye jāte the . In former days the thief's hand used to be cut off. (Eastern governments). 2. Ek ajab maiṅ dekhī nār, achchhe mukh sab uske yār, Sir uskā sab qalam kareṅ, kālā mūṅh kar āge dhareṅ . Riddle. Ans. Qalam a pen. A female strange I saw, all hearts her fair face won, Her head cut off, black faced she's set before each one. qalam lagānā , To set down slips or cuttings. qalam mārnā , v. a. To draw the pen across. qalam yārī denā , v. a. To live by one's pen. yak-qalam , adv. With a stroke of the pen; at once. qalmeṅ chhoṛnā , v. a. To leave some hairs in the upper part of the beard.
s.m. A chameleon ( = bū-qalamūn, q.v.). adj. & s.m. See s.v. bū. s.f. = būʼā, and bū-bū, qq.v. s.f. Sister, dear sister; father's sister, aunt (syn. phūphū; phūʼā; cf. bū-bū).

Meṛe jī kā kales miṭāë de, ri ! Koī maike kī rāh batāë de rī! Wom. song. I pray you, Sir! would some one but my father tell! My anguish wipe, direct me where my parents dwell! 2. Contention; ford ( jhagṛā ). kales k. , v. a. 1. To affect; pain. 2. To strive with; struggle against; to fight. Merā kahnā mān pyāre! kyon jā hai pardes? To suffer grief or pain; to mourn. kales machānā , v. n. To create dissensions, quarrels, etc. kales-hāyā , n. m. kales-hāī , n. f. A. quarrelsome person.

def: s.m. A die; the game of dice personified; name of the last and worst of the four yugas or ages, the present or iron age (according to the Hindūs), the age of vice (the Kali or fourth age contains 432,000 years of men, and is supposed to begin on the 18th February 3102 B.C.; at the end of this yuga the world is to be destroyed); — dissension, strife, contention, quarrel
more: n. f. S. कलिका. 1. A bud; blossom. Tāgā ṭūṭe phir juṛe, kalī ṭūṭ kumlāë, Man kā ṭūṭā ādmī dhore dhore jāë . The broken thread will join, slow fades nipped flowers unblown, The heart of man may break yet brokenly live on. 2. A feather. Kabūtar kī kalī ṭūṭī . 3. Quick lime; wet-lime. 4. A piece in a native coat. 5. A kind of pipe for smoking. kalī barābar . ( Bot .) Regular. kalī be-barābar . ( Bot .) Irregular. kalī chaṛhī-huī . ( Bot .) Imbricated. kalī kā chūnā . Limestone. kaliyāṅ ānā, kaliyānā , v. n. 1. To bud; blossom. 2. To get new feathers. Paṭṭhā kalyā rahā hai. ek kaliyā, milā huā . ( Boṭ .) Mono-sepalous. kachchī yā band kalī , n. f. 1. A. pomegranate bud. 2. A virgin.
s. f. (dakh. s. m.) 1. A bud, an unblown flower, blossom. 2. Quicklime. 3. कलि The kali-yug, or fourth age of the world, according to the Hindūs.
subs: vulg. kal-kāl, s.m. The time or age of Kali, the fourth age (in the Hindū Mythology)
s.m. An epithet of any poet of the fourth or Kali-yug
vulg. kal-mal, s.m. Impurity, guilt, sin

Black soil. kālī mirch , n. f. 1. Black …

1. adj. f. Black. کالي مرچ kālī mirch, s. f. Black pepper. 2. s. f. The Hecate of the Hindūs, and wife of Siva, to whom human sacrifices are offered. 3. The Nile. 4. Ink. 5. s. m. Tir. n. f. S. कलह A quarrelsome woman. 1. Tujh kalihārī nār se maiṅ dukhiyā hūṅ āp, Jo tū ghar se nikal jāë miṭeṅ janam ke pāp . Play. My life's a burden made by such a wrangling jade, Would you the house but leave't would be a life's reprieve. 2. Jis ghar ho nārī kalihārī us purkhā kī saë at khuārī . Rus. song. A wrangling woman for a spouse brings ruin on her husband's house. 3. Kad yoh sāsū, rāṅḍ maregī? pothī khol, are pāṅḍe! Jib lag jīve yoh kalihārī nā ghar-vās base, pāṅde . Rus. Wom. Widowed be she! when will this mother-in-law be dead? See, holy father, in thy book! for while this wrangling jade's to live this house wont prosper, priest.

def: s.m. See s.v. کلي kali. s.f. A woollen carpet; tapestry ( = qālīn).
more: n. m. S. कलयुग The 4th or iron age comprising a period of 432,000 years, supposed to have begun on Friday, the 18th of February, or 3,102 years before the Christian era. 1. Beṭā khāve, bāp lakhāve, Kaliug apnā bal dikhlāve . Prov. The son feeds well, the father just looks on, the iron age dominion hath. 2. Yā kal'jug kī kāmnī chaleṅ parchhāīn kī chhāṅ, Mūṅh pe pallo ger ke kareṅ ughāṛī bāṅh . Kunḍlī. Fair damsels of this iron age even of their shadows vain, Their faces though close veiled they keep their arms all bare remain. kal-jug kā parbhāo! The influence of the iron age.
s. m. The fourth or iron age, comprising a period of 432,000 years, which is supposed to have begun about 3,000 years before the Christian era.
